objective: Final pre-build QA review of metaswarm and SwarmMesh readiness using the synced launcher and fixed Agent Pulse.
blockers: Task tool returned synchronous findings without background agent ids, so launch_started telemetry was recorded without agent_id fields.
consensus_notes: Consensus: collaboration bus scaffolding exists in bin/stampede.sh and malformed-ledger tolerance is partially designed.
Consensus: premium model policy, banned-model list, Depth Guard, and launch-proof telemetry are strongly specified for commanders.
Consensus: documentation/spec drift exists across README.md and skills/SKILL.md for default model, max agent count, and install paths.
Consensus: recovery behavior has a gap because stale claimed work is only requeued after all panes are non-live, allowing single-pane hangs to stall progress.
conflict_notes: Conflict: one worker found collaboration-bus concepts absent from docs, while launcher audit found concrete collab ledgers in bin/stampede.sh; resolved as implementation exists but docs do not name or explain it consistently.
Conflict: some findings framed Agent Pulse as missing; resolved as live counts exist in monitor/state summaries, but an explicit Agent Pulse contract/name is not consistently documented or enforced.
main_findings: bin/stampede.sh creates collab protocol.json and five append-only ledgers, but lacks schema/health enforcement for commander writes.
agents/stampede-commander.agent.md enforces premium child models, banned-model policy, Depth Guard, and launch-proof telemetry, but lacks a detailed recovery/requeue contract.
README.md and skills/SKILL.md drift on default model, max worker count, and agent install path, weakening protocol alignment.
bin/stampede-monitor.sh tolerates missing/malformed ledgers for counts, but orphan recovery only occurs when no panes are live.
